title: Intensely Floral Indian Chicken Wings Paratha
description: A monsoon Indian dish featuring sashimi-cut chicken wings in a pillowy paratha, served in a bamboo tray, with intensely floral flavors and FODMAP-friendly ingredients.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Mix the jasmine rice flour, ghee, rose petal jam, orange blossom water, salt, and black pepper in a large bowl.
Add the sashimi-cut chicken wings to the bowl and toss to coat evenly.
Knead the mixture for 5 minutes until it forms a smooth dough.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle.
Place a few pieces of the coated chicken wings in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the chicken wings to form a paratha and press the edges to seal.
Place the parathas in a bamboo tray and bake for 20 minutes.
Serve the parathas hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro.
